beats.activity: Wake up, black coffee, read the Seattle Times at the kitchen table.
beats.reasoning: Mike's disciplined morning routine—early riser, no-nonsense, sets the tone for the day. Newspaper habit reflects his methodical nature.

beats.activity: Drive from home to a familiar diner for breakfast.
beats.location_name: Matt's in the Market (Pike Place Market)
beats.reasoning: Early morning drive across town; Mike prefers diners and routine. Pike Place diner fits his no-frills aesthetic.

beats.activity: Breakfast at the diner: eggs, toast, more coffee. Observes the morning crowd.
beats.location_name: Matt's in the Market (Pike Place Market)
beats.reasoning: Mike's preference for diners over fancy places; his observant nature means he notices the market's early rhythms.

beats.activity: Drive to Wallingford office for a morning client consultation.
beats.reasoning: Saturday work for a PI/security consultant; Mike's disciplined approach means he keeps weekend appointments. Across-town drive budgeted generously.

beats.activity: Meet with a client at the office; review case files and surveillance notes.
beats.reasoning: Private investigator work; methodical review of evidence fits his meticulous personality.

beats.activity: Return home; listen to the USA vs Wales FIFA match on the radio during the drive.
beats.reasoning: Mike is not a die-hard sports fan, but the match timing (11am) aligns with his return home; he catches it passively on the radio rather than sitting down to watch. Reflects his solitary, disciplined nature.

beats.activity: Light lunch at home; continues listening to the match quietly in the background.
beats.reasoning: Solitary, low-key engagement with the world event; Mike doesn't prioritize sports but doesn't avoid it either. Lunch at home is consistent with his routine.

beats.activity: Drive to the park to watch his granddaughter play.
beats.reasoning: Saturday afternoon ritual; Mike's loyalty to family is fierce. Sunny weather makes this an ideal day for the park. Reasonable travel time from Downtown.

beats.activity: Sit on a bench and watch his granddaughter play. Takes in the sunny day, observes other families.
beats.reasoning: Core part of Mike's weekly routine; his observant nature means he watches everything around the park, not just the grandchild. Sunny weather makes this comfortable.

beats.activity: Drive back home through the city; takes the long way to think.
beats.reasoning: Mike's habit of taking solitary drives to think; allows him to decompress after family time. Generous time budgeted for a reflective route.

beats.activity: Prepare a simple dinner; eat quietly while reviewing case notes on his laptop.
beats.reasoning: No-nonsense dinner; Mike combines meals with work, reflecting his disciplined and methodical approach. Avoids fancy restaurants in favor of home cooking.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: read a paperback thriller, sip black coffee, reflect on the day.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down at home before sleep; Mike's quiet, solitary nature means he spends evenings in contemplation. Positions him at home before the sleep beat.
Seven hours of solid sleep; Mike's disciplined routine includes early bedtime to support his 6:15am wake-up. Sleep beat spans overnight as required.

diary: Started early with the Times and black coffee—old habits die hard. Matt's was packed with the usual Monday crowd; I watched the morning rush like I used to watch the streets, just different now. The client meeting in Wallingford went smooth enough, though the case file's got more holes than I'd like. Drove home listening to the USA-Wales match on the radio, something about that kept my mind off things. Had lunch, then headed over to Volunteer Park to watch my granddaughter play. She didn't know I was there at first, but when she spotted me on the bench, that smile made the whole morning worth it. The sun was warm, the park was full of families doing normal things. Took the long way home after, just needed to think. Spent the evening with my thriller and coffee, going through case notes, but mostly just sitting with the quiet.
thought_process: The light rail closure didn't affect me much—I know the city well enough to work around it, and driving gives me thinking time anyway. I went back home for the match because some things feel important to hold onto, even if it's just a radio broadcast. Seeing my granddaughter play grounded me; that's the real work now, not the cases. The client consultation was routine, but I caught myself being impatient with the details—maybe I'm losing the hunger for it, or maybe I'm just learning when things matter and when they don't. The long drive home was necessary. I needed to process, to settle. By evening, I was content in that quiet way you get when you've moved through a full day and nothing went wrong.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
